title: Big Tech Is Buying Up Open Source... Vite Is Next
summary: Cloudflare acquired VoidZero, the company behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, and Oxc. The video discusses a wider trend of big tech companies acquiring developer tooling companies and explores Cloudflare's motivations, including potential AI integration, alongside concerns about open source consolidation.
writeup: Better Stack examines Cloudflare's acquisition of VoidZero, the company behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, and Oxc. The video frames the deal as part of a broader pattern of large technology firms buying up developer tooling, and it raises concerns about consolidation and the role of AI infrastructure in driving these moves. The takeaway: control of widely used open source build and test tools is shifting toward a single corporate owner, with implications for how that tooling evolves.
